paper_title: Stable Isotope Analysis of Micromeryx Tooth Enamel

methods.method_name: Stable Isotope Analysis of Tooth Enamel
methods.description: Process for sampling mammalian tooth enamel and preparing it for stable carbon and oxygen isotope analysis (δ13C, δ18O CO3, δ18O PO4) to reconstruct palaeoecological preferences and past environmental/climatic conditions.

steps.description: Tooth enamel was sampled using a rotary drill with a diamond-tipped dental burr under a microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Sample tooth enamel using rotary drill and microscope

steps.description: Recover enamel from an area of the tooth as large as possible to avoid seasonal bias in the time of mineralisation.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sufficient tooth enamel sample size

steps.description: Sampling protocol and chemical treatment follows Domingo et al. [24] for carbonate and phosphate in bioapatite.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform chemical treatment on tooth enamel sample for carbonate and phosphate analysis
outputs: Chemically treated tooth enamel sample (carbonate and phosphate fractions)

steps.description: A total of 37 samples were analysed for δ 13 C and δ 18 O CO3.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze tooth enamel samples for stable carbon (δ13C) and oxygen carbonate (δ18O CO3) isotopes
inputs: Chemically treated tooth enamel samples (carbonate fraction)

steps.description: whereas 30 tooth enamel samples were analysed for δ 18 O PO4.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze tooth enamel samples for stable oxygen phosphate (δ18O PO4) isotopes
inputs: Chemically treated tooth enamel samples (phosphate fraction)

methods.description: Method to check for post-mortem alteration in fossil bioapatite by comparing the difference between δ18O CO3 and δ18O PO4 values to expected modern values.

steps.description: When both δ 18 O CO3 and δ 18 O PO4 values have been determined...
steps.searchable_summary: Ensure both δ18O CO3 and δ18O PO4 values are available for a sample
outputs: Paired δ18O CO3 and δ18O PO4 data

steps.description: ...post mortem alteration can be checked by calculating the difference between δ 18 O CO3 and δ 18 O PO4 (Δ 18 O CO3 -PO4 = δ 18 O CO3 − δ 18 O PO4 ).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the difference between δ18O CO3 and δ18O PO4

steps.description: This follows the premise that bioapatite in modern mammals have a restricted difference of~8.6-9.1‰ [22,23].
steps.searchable_summary: Compare calculated Δ18O CO3 -PO4 to the expected range for unaltered bioapatite (8.6-9.1‰)
outputs: Comparison result (e.g., within range, outside range)

steps.description: Δ 18 O CO3 -PO4 mean value for the whole dataset of Micromeryx samples is 8.6 ± 0.8‰, and therefore, it is within the expected range for unaltered bioapatite.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess diagenetic alteration based on the comparison result
outputs: Assessment of diagenetic alteration (e.g., unaltered, altered)

methods.description: Methods for estimating Mean Annual Precipitation (MAP) and Mean Annual Temperature (MAT) from tooth enamel stable isotope values using published equations.

steps.description: Mean annual precipitation (MAP) from Micromeryx tooth enamel δ 13 C was estimated following Kohn [54].
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate Mean Annual Precipitation (MAP) from δ13C using Kohn [54] method

steps.description: ...both with and without altitude and latitude correction.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply altitude and latitude corrections to MAP estimation

steps.description: Estimated mean annual temperature (MAT) was also calculated following a two-stepped method and using published equations [74,75].
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Estimated Mean Annual Temperature (MAT) using a two-stepped method and published equations

methods.description: Statistical procedure used to compare stable isotope values between different groups (Micromeryx morphotypes).

steps.description: Significant differences in δ 13 C values have been only found between morphotypes 1 and 2 of Micromeryx.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare δ13C values between Micromeryx morphotypes 1 and 2
Perform a t-test to assess statistical significance of the difference

methods.description: Describes the process of obtaining and selecting fossil Micromeryx tooth enamel samples from specific paleontological localities for analysis.

steps.description: Micromeryx specimens analysed in this paper are housed in the Institut Català de Paleontologia Miquel Crusafont (ICP) in Sabadell (Spain).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain Micromeryx specimens from the ICP repository

steps.description: They come from 11 fossil localities in the 250m-thick ACM composite stratigraphic sequence [5,15], in els Hostalets de Pierola (Vallès-Penedès Basin, Catalonia, Spain; Fig. 1a-c).
steps.searchable_summary: Select specimens originating from specific fossil localities within the ACM sequence
inputs: List of target localities (11 localities in ACM sequence)

steps.description: These localities are restricted areas corresponding to a single layer (or stratigraphic horizon), usually between 0.2 and 1.0 m in thickness, from which fossil remains were collected (see Alba et al. [5] for further details).
steps.searchable_summary: Ensure specimens were collected from specific stratigraphic layers
outputs: Verified Micromeryx specimens from specific layers

steps.description: We analyse patterns of tooth wear and enamel stable isotope composition... of three different Micromeryx morphotypes (termed morphotypes 1, 2 and 3).
steps.searchable_summary: Select specimens belonging to three specific Micromeryx morphotypes (1, 2, 3)
